short-dialog.dialogue:
M: Can I help you?
W: Yes, I'm looking for a book on history.
short-dialog.question: What is the woman looking for?
short-dialog.analysis: 关键词 'book on history' 直接回答了问题

short-dialog.dialogue:
M: What time does the train leave?
W: It leaves at 3:30, but we should arrive 15 minutes early.
short-dialog.question: When should they arrive at the station?
short-dialog.analysis: 火车 3:30 出发，应该提前 15 分钟到达，即 3:15

short-dialog.dialogue:
W: Did you finish your homework?
M: Not yet, I still need another hour.
short-dialog.question: What is the man doing?
short-dialog.analysis: 男士说还需要一小时完成作业，说明他正在做作业

short-dialog.dialogue:
M: How much is this shirt?
W: It's $50, but it's on sale for 20% off today.
short-dialog.question: How much will the man pay?

short-dialog.dialogue:
W: Are you coming to the party tonight?
M: I'd love to, but I have to work late.
short-dialog.question: Why can't the man go to the party?
short-dialog.analysis: 男士明确说必须工作到很晚

short-dialog.dialogue:
M: Where is the nearest bank?
W: Go straight for two blocks, then turn left.
short-dialog.question: What is the man asking for?
short-dialog.analysis: 男士询问最近的银行在哪里，是在问路

short-dialog.dialogue:
W: What's your major?
M: I'm studying computer science, but I'm interested in art too.
short-dialog.question: What is the man's major?
short-dialog.analysis: 男士明确说他的专业是计算机科学

short-dialog.dialogue:
M: Did you see the movie last night?
W: No, I was too tired. I fell asleep at 9 PM.
short-dialog.question: Why didn't the woman see the movie?
short-dialog.analysis: 女士说她太累了，9 点就睡着了

short-dialog.dialogue:
W: How often do you exercise?
M: I try to go to the gym three times a week.
short-dialog.question: How often does the man exercise?
short-dialog.analysis: 男士说他尽量每周去健身房三次

short-dialog.dialogue:
M: Is this seat taken?
W: No, you can sit here.
short-dialog.question: What does the woman mean?
short-dialog.analysis: 女士说可以坐这里，说明座位是空的

long-dialog.dialogue:
M: Hi, Sarah. Are you ready for the presentation tomorrow?
W: Not really, John. I'm still working on the slides. How about you?
M: I finished mine yesterday. But I'm worried about the Q&A session.
W: Don't worry. The professor said the questions would be about the main points.
M: That's good to know. Did you review the data from last month?
W: Yes, I did. The sales increased by 15% compared to the previous month.
M: Great! That's a key point we should mention.
W: Absolutely. I'll make sure to highlight it in my presentation.

questions.question: What are the speakers mainly discussing?
questions.analysis: 对话围绕明天的演讲准备展开

questions.question: What is the woman still working on?
questions.analysis: 女士说她还在做幻灯片

questions.question: By how much did the sales increase?
questions.analysis: 女士明确说销售额增长了 15%

long-dialog.dialogue:
W: Excuse me, could you help me find a book?
M: Of course. What book are you looking for?
W: I need 'Introduction to Psychology' by Dr. Smith.
M: Let me check our catalog. Yes, we have it. It's in the psychology section, shelf B3.
W: Thank you. Can I borrow it for two weeks?
M: Yes, but you can renew it online if you need more time.
W: That's convenient. How many books can I borrow at once?
M: Students can borrow up to 10 books at a time.
W: Perfect. I'll also need some journals on the same topic.
M: The journals are in the reference section. They can't be borrowed, but you can photocopy the articles you need.

questions.question: Where does this conversation take place?
questions.analysis: 对话中提到借阅、目录、书架等，说明是在图书馆

questions.question: Where can the woman find the book?
questions.analysis: 男士明确说书在心理学区域 B3 书架

questions.question: How many books can a student borrow at once?
questions.analysis: 男士说学生一次可以借 10 本书

long-dialog.dialogue:
M: Good morning, Ms. Chen. I'm here for my job interview.
W: Good morning, Mr. Wang. Please have a seat. Let me review your resume first.
M: Thank you. I'm excited about this opportunity.
W: I can see you have three years of experience in marketing. Can you tell me about your biggest achievement?
M: In my previous company, I led a campaign that increased sales by 30% in six months.
W: That's impressive. What strategies did you use?
M: We focused on social media marketing and targeted advertising.
W: Great. Why do you want to work for our company?
M: I admire your innovative approach to marketing, and I believe I can contribute to your team.
W: Excellent. Do you have any questions for me?
M: Yes, what does a typical day look like in this position?

questions.question: What is the purpose of this conversation?
questions.analysis: 男士明确说是来参加面试的

questions.question: By how much did the man's campaign increase sales?
questions.analysis: 男士说他的活动使销售额增长了 30%

questions.question: What marketing strategies did the man use?
questions.analysis: 男士说他们专注于社交媒体营销和定向广告

long-dialog.dialogue:
W: Hi, Tom. Did you hear about the company retreat next month?
M: Yes, I did. Are you planning to go?
W: Definitely! It's going to be at a beach resort for three days.
M: That sounds amazing. What activities are planned?
W: There will be team-building exercises, workshops, and of course, free time to enjoy the beach.
M: Will families be allowed to attend?
W: No, it's just for employees. But there will be plenty of networking opportunities.
M: That's good. I've been wanting to meet people from other departments.
W: Same here. By the way, have you registered yet?
M: Not yet. What's the deadline?
W: The deadline is next Friday. You should register soon because spots are limited.

questions.question: Where will the company retreat take place?
questions.analysis: 女士说 retreat 将在海滩度假村举行

questions.question: How long will the retreat last?
questions.analysis: 女士明确说是三天

questions.question: When is the registration deadline?
questions.analysis: 女士说截止日期是下周五

long-dialog.dialogue:
M: Professor Li, do you have a minute?
W: Of course, David. What can I help you with?
M: I'm having trouble with my research paper. I can't seem to find enough sources.
W: What's your topic?
M: I'm writing about the impact of social media on mental health.
W: That's a very current topic. Have you tried searching the academic databases?
M: Yes, I've used Google Scholar, but I need more peer-reviewed articles.
W: I recommend using PsycINFO and PubMed. They have extensive psychology research.
M: Thank you! Also, how long should the paper be?
W: The requirement is 15-20 pages, not including references.
M: And when is it due?
W: It's due on December 15th. You still have three weeks.

questions.question: What is the student's problem?
questions.analysis: 学生说他找不到足够的资料

questions.question: What is the student's research topic?
questions.analysis: 学生说他的主题是社交媒体对心理健康的影响

questions.question: When is the paper due?
questions.analysis: 教授说论文截止日期是 12 月 15 日

long-dialog.dialogue:
W: Welcome to our restaurant. Do you have a reservation?
M: Yes, under the name Johnson, for four people.
W: Let me check... Yes, we have your table ready. Right this way.
M: Thank you. Could we sit by the window?
W: Of course. Here are your menus. Can I get you something to drink while you decide?
M: I'll have a glass of red wine. What do you recommend?
W: Our house wine is excellent. It's a Cabernet from California.
M: Perfect. I'll try that. My friends will order when they arrive.
W: Very good. Your server will be with you shortly.
M: By the way, do you have any vegetarian options?
W: Yes, we have several vegetarian dishes. They're marked with a 'V' on the menu.

questions.question: How many people is the reservation for?
questions.analysis: 男士说预订了四个人的位置

questions.question: Where does the man want to sit?
questions.analysis: 男士请求坐在窗边

questions.question: What drink does the man order?
questions.analysis: 男士说要一杯红酒

long-dialog.dialogue:
M: Hi, I'd like to return this jacket, please.
W: Do you have the receipt?
M: Yes, here it is. I bought it last week.
W: I see. What's the reason for the return?
M: It's too small. I tried it on at home and it doesn't fit.
W: No problem. Would you like to exchange it for a larger size or get a refund?
M: I'd like to exchange it for a size larger, please.
W: Let me check if we have it in stock... Yes, we do. I'll get it for you.
M: Thank you. Also, can I try it on before I take it?
W: Of course. The fitting room is right over there.
M: Great. If it doesn't fit, can I come back tomorrow?
W: Yes, you have 30 days to exchange or return items.

questions.question: Why does the man want to return the jacket?
questions.analysis: 男士说夹克太小了

questions.question: What does the man decide to do?
questions.analysis: 男士说要换大一码的

questions.question: How long does the customer have to return items?
questions.analysis: 女士说有 30 天的退换货期限

long-dialog.dialogue:
W: Good afternoon, Dr. Brown's office. How can I help you?
M: Hi, I'd like to make an appointment with Dr. Brown.
W: Is this your first visit?
M: Yes, it is. I was referred by my friend.
W: Okay. What's your name and phone number?
M: It's Michael Chen, and my number is 555-1234.
W: Thank you, Mr. Chen. What's the reason for your visit?
M: I've been having back pain for the past two weeks.
W: I see. Dr. Brown has availability on Tuesday at 2 PM or Thursday at 10 AM.
M: Tuesday at 2 PM works better for me.
W: Perfect. Please arrive 15 minutes early to fill out some forms.
M: Will do. Do I need to bring anything?
W: Just your ID and insurance card. Dr. Brown will see you then.

questions.question: Why is the man calling?
questions.analysis: 男士说要预约 Dr. Brown

questions.question: What is the man's problem?
questions.analysis: 男士说他背痛已经两周了

questions.question: When is the appointment scheduled?
questions.analysis: 男士选择了周二下午 2 点

long-dialog.dialogue:
M: Hey, Lisa. Are you going to the career fair next week?
W: Yes, I am. I've been preparing my resume for weeks.
M: That's great. Which companies are you interested in?
W: I'm mainly interested in tech companies like Google and Microsoft.
M: Those are competitive. Have you practiced your interview skills?
W: Yes, I've been doing mock interviews with my roommate.
M: Smart move. Do you have any questions prepared to ask the recruiters?
W: Definitely. I want to ask about their training programs and company culture.
M: Good idea. I'm more interested in startups myself.
W: Startups can be exciting. You might have more opportunities to grow quickly.
M: Exactly. That's why I'm targeting smaller companies.
W: Well, good luck! Let me know how it goes.

questions.question: What event are the speakers discussing?
questions.analysis: 对话围绕招聘会展开

questions.question: What type of companies is the woman interested in?
questions.analysis: 女士说她主要对科技公司感兴趣

questions.question: How has the woman prepared for interviews?
questions.analysis: 女士说她和室友进行了模拟面试

long-dialog.dialogue:
W: Hi, I'm calling to inquire about your English courses.
M: Welcome! We offer various courses for different levels. What level are you?
W: I think I'm intermediate. I can have basic conversations but want to improve.
M: Then our Intermediate Communication course would be perfect for you.
W: How long is the course?
M: It's 12 weeks, with two classes per week, each class is 90 minutes.
W: That sounds manageable. How much does it cost?
M: It's $600 for the full course, but we have a 10% discount if you register this week.
W: That's a good deal. Do you offer evening classes?
M: Yes, we have evening classes at 6 PM and 7:30 PM.
W: Perfect. I work during the day. How do I register?
M: You can register online or come to our office. We're open Monday to Saturday.

questions.question: What is the woman interested in?
questions.analysis: 女士打电话询问英语课程

questions.question: How long is the course?
questions.analysis: 男士说课程是 12 周

questions.question: What discount is offered this week?
questions.analysis: 男士说本周注册有 10% 的折扣

passage.passage: The Internet has revolutionized the way we communicate and access information. In just a few decades, it has transformed from a small network connecting academic institutions to a global system that billions of people use daily. Today, we can instantly connect with anyone around the world, access vast amounts of information, and conduct business online. However, this rapid growth has also brought challenges. Privacy concerns, misinformation, and cybercrime are serious issues that society must address. Despite these challenges, the Internet continues to evolve, with new technologies like artificial intelligence and the Internet of Things promising even more changes in the future.

questions.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
questions.analysis: 文章主要讲述互联网的影响和变革

questions.question: What was the Internet originally used for?
questions.analysis: 文章提到互联网最初是连接学术机构的网络

questions.question: Which challenge is NOT mentioned in the passage?
questions.analysis: 文章提到了隐私、虚假信息和网络犯罪，但没有提到成瘾问题

passage.passage: Climate change is one of the most pressing issues of our time. Scientists have observed rising global temperatures, melting ice caps, and more frequent extreme weather events. These changes are largely caused by human activities, particularly the burning of fossil fuels that release greenhouse gases into the atmosphere. To address this problem, countries around the world are taking action. Many are investing in renewable energy sources like solar and wind power. Others are implementing policies to reduce carbon emissions. Individuals can also contribute by making small changes in their daily lives, such as using public transportation, reducing waste, and conserving energy. While the challenge is significant, collective action can make a difference.

questions.question: What is causing climate change according to the passage?
questions.analysis: 文章明确说气候变化主要是人类活动造成的

questions.question: Which renewable energy sources are mentioned?
questions.analysis: 文章提到了太阳能和风能

questions.question: What can individuals do to help?
questions.analysis: 文章说个人可以通过日常生活中的小改变来贡献

passage.passage: Education is the foundation of personal and societal development. Throughout history, societies that valued education have tended to prosper more than those that didn't. In today's knowledge economy, education is more important than ever. It not only provides individuals with skills and knowledge but also helps them develop critical thinking and problem-solving abilities. Modern education goes beyond traditional classroom learning. Online courses, vocational training, and lifelong learning programs offer flexible options for people of all ages. However, access to quality education remains unequal in many parts of the world. Efforts to improve educational equity are essential for building a better future.

questions.question: Why is education important according to the passage?
options: It provides skills and develops thinking
questions.analysis: 文章说教育提供技能知识并培养批判性思维

questions.question: What forms of modern education are mentioned?
questions.analysis: 文章提到了在线课程、职业培训和终身学习

questions.question: What problem still exists in education?
questions.analysis: 文章说优质教育的获取仍然不平等

passage.passage: Physical exercise is essential for maintaining good health. Regular activity strengthens the heart, improves circulation, and helps maintain a healthy weight. It also has mental health benefits, reducing stress and improving mood. Experts recommend at least 150 minutes of moderate exercise per week for adults. This can include activities like brisk walking, cycling, or swimming. For those with busy schedules, even short bursts of exercise can be beneficial. Taking the stairs instead of the elevator, walking during lunch breaks, or doing home workouts are all effective ways to stay active. The key is to find activities you enjoy and make them a regular part of your routine.

questions.question: How much exercise do experts recommend per week?
questions.analysis: 文章说专家建议每周至少 150 分钟中等强度运动

questions.question: Which activity is NOT mentioned as exercise?
questions.analysis: 文章提到了快走、骑自行车和游泳，但没有提到跑步

questions.question: What is the key to maintaining an exercise routine?
questions.analysis: 文章说关键是找到你喜欢的活动并使其成为常规

passage.passage: Artificial intelligence is transforming many industries. In healthcare, AI helps doctors diagnose diseases more accurately. In transportation, self-driving cars are being developed to reduce accidents. In finance, AI algorithms detect fraudulent transactions. However, the rise of AI also raises concerns about job displacement. Some jobs may be automated, while new types of jobs will be created. Experts suggest that workers should focus on developing skills that complement AI, such as creativity, emotional intelligence, and complex problem-solving. The future of work will likely involve collaboration between humans and AI systems, with each doing what they do best.

questions.question: Which industry is NOT mentioned in the passage?
questions.analysis: 文章提到了医疗、交通和金融，但没有提到农业

questions.question: What concern does AI raise?
questions.analysis: 文章提到 AI 引发了对工作被取代的担忧

questions.question: What skills should workers develop?
questions.analysis: 文章说应该发展与 AI 互补的技能，如创造力、情商等

passage.passage: Sleep is crucial for physical and mental health. During sleep, the body repairs itself, and the brain processes information from the day. Adults typically need 7-9 hours of sleep per night. However, many people don't get enough sleep due to stress, technology use, or irregular schedules. Lack of sleep can lead to various health problems, including weakened immune system, weight gain, and difficulty concentrating. To improve sleep quality, experts recommend maintaining a regular sleep schedule, creating a comfortable sleep environment, and avoiding screens before bedtime. Good sleep habits are an investment in long-term health and well-being.

questions.question: How much sleep do adults typically need?
questions.analysis: 文章说成年人通常需要 7-9 小时睡眠

questions.question: Which is NOT a consequence of lack of sleep?
questions.analysis: 文章说睡眠不足会导致注意力难以集中，而不是改善

questions.question: What is recommended to improve sleep?
questions.analysis: 文章建议保持规律的睡眠时间表

passage.passage: Cultural diversity enriches societies in many ways. Different cultures bring unique perspectives, traditions, and innovations. In multicultural cities, people can experience foods, arts, and celebrations from around the world. This exposure helps break down stereotypes and promotes understanding between different groups. However, cultural diversity can also present challenges. Language barriers and different social norms can sometimes lead to misunderstandings. Successful multicultural societies invest in programs that promote integration while respecting cultural differences. They recognize that diversity is a strength that can drive creativity, economic growth, and social progress.

questions.question: What is the main benefit of cultural diversity?
questions.analysis: 文章说不同文化带来独特的视角、传统和创新

questions.question: What challenge is mentioned?
questions.analysis: 文章提到语言障碍和不同的社会规范可能导致误解

questions.question: What do successful multicultural societies do?
questions.analysis: 文章说成功的多元文化社会在尊重文化差异的同时促进融合

passage.passage: Entrepreneurship plays a vital role in economic development. Entrepreneurs create new businesses, which generate jobs and drive innovation. Many of today's largest companies started as small startups founded by entrepreneurs. However, starting a business is risky. Many new businesses fail within the first few years. Successful entrepreneurs typically have certain characteristics: they are creative, persistent, and willing to take calculated risks. They also need to understand their market, manage finances effectively, and build strong teams. Governments can support entrepreneurship through policies that reduce barriers to starting businesses and provide access to funding and resources.

questions.question: What do entrepreneurs contribute to the economy?
questions.analysis: 文章说企业家创造企业，产生就业并推动创新

questions.question: What is a characteristic of successful entrepreneurs?
questions.analysis: 文章说成功的企业家具有创造力、坚持并愿意承担计算过的风险

questions.question: How can governments support entrepreneurship?
options: By reducing barriers and providing funding
questions.analysis: 文章说政府可以通过减少创业障碍和提供资金资源来支持创业

passage.passage: Water is essential for all life on Earth. Despite covering most of the planet's surface, only a small percentage of water is fresh and available for human use. Many regions face water scarcity due to overuse, pollution, and climate change. Agriculture accounts for about 70% of global freshwater consumption. In water-scarce areas, people are developing innovative solutions. These include water-efficient irrigation systems, wastewater recycling, and desalination plants. Conservation is also important. Simple actions like fixing leaks, using water-efficient appliances, and reducing water waste can make a significant difference. Protecting water resources is crucial for sustainable development.

questions.question: What percentage of water is fresh and available for human use?
questions.analysis: 文章说只有一小百分比的水是淡水且可供人类使用

questions.question: Which sector uses the most freshwater?
questions.analysis: 文章说农业占全球淡水消耗的约 70%

questions.question: What is NOT mentioned as a water conservation solution?
questions.analysis: 文章提到了节水灌溉、废水回收和海水淡化，但没有提到建更多水坝

passage.passage: Reading is a valuable habit that offers numerous benefits. It improves vocabulary, enhances critical thinking, and reduces stress. Regular reading can also improve memory and concentration. In our digital age, many people spend more time on social media than reading books. However, research shows that deep reading of books provides cognitive benefits that quick scrolling through social media cannot match. Parents can encourage reading in children by reading to them from an early age and creating a home environment rich in books. Libraries and book clubs are also excellent resources for developing a love of reading. Making time for reading is an investment in personal growth and mental well-being.

questions.question: Which is NOT a benefit of reading mentioned in the passage?
questions.analysis: 文章提到了词汇、批判性思维和减压，但没有提到增强体力

questions.question: How does reading books compare to social media?
questions.analysis: 文章说深度阅读书籍提供社交媒体无法比拟的认知益处

questions.question: How can parents encourage reading in children?
options: Read to them from an early age
questions.analysis: 文章说父母可以通过从小给孩子读书来鼓励阅读
